title: Proclamation Imposing a Temporary Import Surcharge to Address Fundamental International Payments Problems
summary: This proclamation, issued by President Donald J. Trump, declares the imposition of a temporary import surcharge on goods imported into the United States to address fundamental international payments problems. It cites authority from the U.S. Constitution and federal law, including sections 122 and 301 of title 3, United States Code, and section 604, among others, and outlines the scope and basis for the surcharge as well as related implementing measures.

title: Continuation of Suspension of Duty-Free De Minimis Treatment for All Countries
summary: This presidential action extends the policy that suspends duty-free de minimis treatment for shipments from all countries. It cites authority under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974, as amended. The action maintains the current framework in which shipments that would otherwise qualify for duty-free treatment remain liable for applicable duties.

title: Presidential Action Terminating Specific Tariff Measures
summary: This presidential action cites authorities including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974 to terminate certain tariff actions and end specified tariff measures. The action reflects the President's use of statutory powers to adjust trade-related measures.

title: Executive Order to Promote National Defense by Ensuring Adequate Supply of Elemental Phosphorus and Glyphosate-Based Herbicides
summary: This executive order, issued under the Constitution and the Defense Production Act of 1950, directs federal agencies to identify and secure an adequate supply of elemental phosphorus and glyphosate-based herbicides considered essential for national defense and critical agricultural needs. It outlines policy and findings and authorizes the use of Defense Production Act authorities to prioritize production and allocation, while establishing coordination among relevant agencies to assess supply chains, engage with suppliers, and implement measures to safeguard availability of these materials. The order also sets forth requirements for implementation, reporting, and oversight to monitor progress and ensure compliance with applicable laws.

title: Proclamation by the President of the United States Honoring George Washington on His Birthday, 2026
summary: This proclamation recognizes George Washington as a foundational figure in American history, noting his leadership during the American Revolution and his role in the founding of the United States. It also announces the observance of George Washington's Birthday in 2026 and marks the 250th anniversary of American independence as part of the commemorative activities associated with the birthday.

title: Adjustments to Tariff Duties Addressing Threats Posed by the Government of the Russian Federation
summary: This presidential action invokes the President’s constitutional authority and specified statutes, including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974, to modify duties on imports from the Government of the Russian Federation. The measure is taken to address threats identified by the executive branch and to implement changes to the duties applicable to such imports. The action outlines the legal basis for the modifications, the scope of the duty changes, and the responsibilities of executive-branch agencies for enforcement and administration consistent with applicable law. The document provides the rationale for the modification as part of national security and trade policy measures.

title: Executive Order Establishing a Defense Arms Transfer Strategy Emphasizing Domestic Manufacturing
summary: This executive order establishes a strategy for United States arms transfers that prioritizes American-made military equipment and seeks to maximize exports consistent with national interests. It directs federal agencies to develop and implement policies to promote domestic defense production, coordinate defense trade and export controls, assess foreign procurement risks, and align interagency efforts to leverage the United States' manufacturing leadership in defense exports.
